content.text:
Here's a comprehensive breakdown of the best approaches to integrate AI IDEs with Google NotebookLM:

---

## NotebookLM + AI IDE Integration: Full Picture

### The Core Reality

There is **no public consumer API** for NotebookLM yet. Google has acknowledged demand but given no timeline. Three integration tiers exist:

---

## Approach 1: MCP Server (Best for Claude Code & Cursor)

The `notebooklm-mcp-cli` package wraps the unofficial Python SDK as an **MCP server**, making NotebookLM a first-class tool for Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini CLI.

**How it works in this repo's context:**
- Claude Code (this session) or Cursor can call `notebooklm.create_notebook`, `add_source`, `ask`, `generate_audio`, etc. as MCP tools
- Agentic flows: "research X, create a notebook, import sources, generate study guide" — all in one conversation

**Setup:**
```bash
pip install "notebooklm-py[browser]"
playwright install chromium
notebooklm login            # browser OAuth, saves cookies
notebooklm skill install    # installs skill to ~/.claude/skills/ and ~/.agents/skills/
```

Or via the open skills ecosystem:
```bash
npx skills add teng-lin/notebooklm-py
```

This drops a `SKILL.md` into `~/.claude/skills/notebooklm/` which Claude Code picks up automatically.

---

## Approach 2: Python SDK / CLI in CI or Scripts

Use `notebooklm-py` directly in scripts, shell aliases, or CI pipelines — ideal for this Rust project's workflow:

```bash
# Push today's activtrak report insights to NotebookLM
activtrak report --today --json | notebooklm source add-text --notebook "activtrak-research"

# Bulk-add docs from the repo
notebooklm source add ./docs/*.md --notebook "activtrak-docs"
notebooklm generate quiz --difficulty medium
notebooklm download quiz --format markdown ./output/quiz.md
```

This fits naturally into `scripts/ai/` alongside the existing `atomic-status.sh`, `commit.sh` scripts.

---

## Approach 3: NotebookLM Enterprise API (for org-level use)

If your org has **Gemini Enterprise or Education Premium**, the [official Google Cloud API](https://cloud.google.com/gemini/enterprise/notebooklm-enterprise/docs/api-notebooks) provides:
- Notebook CRUD, source `batchCreate`, audio generation, notebook queries
- VPC Service Controls + CMEK for compliance

This is the right path for team-wide automation or if this becomes a production feature.

---

## Decision Matrix

| Scenario | Best Approach |
|---|---|
| Use Claude Code / Cursor to manage NotebookLM conversationally | `notebooklm skill install` → MCP skill |
| Script-based automation, CI, research pipelines | `notebooklm-py` CLI |
| Enterprise compliance, Google Cloud org | NotebookLM Enterprise API |
| Non-technical / browser-first workflow | Chrome Web Clipper extension |

---

## Recommended Next Step for This Repo

Since Claude Code is primary here, the highest-leverage integration is:

```bash
pip install "notebooklm-py[browser]"
playwright install chromium
notebooklm login
notebooklm skill install
```

After that, you can ask Claude Code to: *"Create a NotebookLM notebook from our docs/ folder and generate a study guide"* — and it will use the skill natively without leaving the IDE session.

The library uses **undocumented Google APIs** (no official consumer API yet), so treat it as prototyping/research tooling rather than production infrastructure.

todos.content: Verify skill is discoverable by Claude Code

input.description: Check available Python and pip versions
Install notebooklm-py with browser support via pip3

input.command: which pipx 2>/dev/null || brew install pipx && pipx ensurepath
input.description: Check/install pipx for isolated package management

content.text: `pipx` is already available. Installing via pipx gives an isolated environment without breaking Homebrew Python.

input.description: Install notebooklm-py via pipx with browser extra

input.command: pipx runpip notebooklm-py show playwright 2>/dev/null; notebooklm --version 2>&1
input.description: Verify notebooklm CLI and check playwright

input.command: pipx runpip notebooklm-py run playwright install chromium 2>&1
input.description: Install Chromium browser inside notebooklm-py's venv
Install Chromium via playwright inside the pipx venv

content.text:
Chromium is downloaded. Now for the auth step — this opens a real browser window so you can sign in with your Google account. Run this in your terminal:

```bash
notebooklm login
```

This will:
1. Open a Chromium window navigating to NotebookLM
2. You complete the Google OAuth flow normally
3. Cookies are saved to `~/.config/notebooklm/` (or similar XDG path)
4. The CLI is ready to use headlessly from that point on

Once you've completed login, come back here and I'll run the skill install step. Go ahead and run it now — I'll wait.
Auth is solid — all checks pass, 20 cookies stored. Now installing the skill.

input.description: Install notebooklm skill for Claude Code and agent dirs

todos.content: Verify skill is discoverable by Claude Code

content.text:
Everything is set up and verified. Here's the full summary:

**Setup complete:**
- `notebooklm-py 0.3.4` installed via pipx
- Chromium (Playwright) downloaded for headless browser
- Auth valid — 20 Google cookies stored at `~/.notebooklm/`
- Skill installed at `~/.claude/skills/notebooklm/SKILL.md`

**What you can now do in this Claude Code session:**

```bash
# List your existing notebooks
notebooklm list

# Create a notebook from activtrak docs
notebooklm create "activtrak-docs"
notebooklm use <notebook_id>
notebooklm source add ./docs/*.md
notebooklm ask "Summarize the architecture"

# Generate a podcast from the codebase docs
notebooklm generate audio "technical deep-dive" --wait
notebooklm download audio ./activtrak-overview.mp3

# Generate study materials
notebooklm generate quiz --difficulty medium
notebooklm download quiz --format markdown ./quiz.md
```

You can also ask me directly in this session things like "create a NotebookLM notebook from our docs/ folder" — the skill is now loaded and I'll use it natively. Start a new chat to pick up the skill fresh if needed.
